name: The Islamic Association of Arabi
mission: PROVIDED RELIGOUS SERVICE AND EDUCATION FOR CHILDREN.BENNETT SUPPORT FOR THE NEEDY BY USE OF ZAKAT MINISTRY.WEDDING AND FUNERAL SERVICE.
theoryOfChange: By providing traditional Islamic education (Hifz/Alim) to American-born Muslims, the organization creates a leadership class capable of serving the community's unique social and legal needs.
scoreSummary: Islamic Association of Arabi shows good impact and moderate alignment, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Affiliated with Halal Monitoring Service (HMS) / Shariah Board of America

zakatClaimEvidence: The Islamic Association of Arabi provides religious services and education for children, supports the needy through the use of Zakat ministry, and offers wedding and funeral services. Benevolence charity to the needy through Zakat Ministry. (Source: https://www.causeiq.com/organizations/islamic-association-of-arabi,721128279/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 39 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: masakin (+5)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 25/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

Zakat-collecting organization holds 39 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

score_details.score_summary: Islamic Association of Arabi shows good impact and moderate alignment, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: The Islamic Association of Arabi provides religious services and education for children, supports the needy through the use of Zakat ministry, and offers wedding and funeral services. Benevolence charity to the needy through Zakat Ministry. (Source: https://www.causeiq.com/organizations/islamic-association-of-arabi,721128279/)

all_citations.claim: The organization is zakat-eligible and maintains a zakat policy for the needy.
The organization reported total revenue of $3,110,855 and a program expense ratio of 96.1%.
The Islamic Association of Arabi operates as Masjid Yaseen and provides religious programming.
The organization provides community services including religious holiday observances.
The association manages educational academies focused on Quran memorization.
The charity offers multiple donation options for community support.
Financial metadata indicates the organization holds 38.8 months of working capital.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score of 44/100, reflecting excellent internal financial efficiency and zakat eligibility, balanced against limited external data verification and impact transparency.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public documentation regarding specific long-term impact metrics and outcome tracking
Enhancement of third-party transparency reporting to improve overall data confidence

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a zakat-eligible institution, the charity aligns well with donors seeking to fulfill religious obligations through the Zakat Ministry and community support services [1][6]. The alignment score reflects a medium donor fit due to its localized religious and cultural focus, supported by a consistent track record of providing wedding and funeral services to the community [2][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The association demonstrates a capacity-building approach through the Al-Hameed Boys Academy and Amatur Rahman Girls Academy, focusing on Quranic literacy and religious education [3][5]. While financial health is robust with a 96.1% program expense ratio and 38.8 months of working capital, there is a lack of third-party verified data regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary outcomes [2][7].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Association of Arabi provides comprehensive religious education and essential social services to the community through its dedicated academies and zakat ministry.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 96.1%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support community services
Strong financial stability with 38.8 months of working capital available
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: Operating as Masjid Yaseen, the Islamic Association of Arabi offers religious services, funeral support, and specialized educational programs including Quran memorization [3][5]. The organization reported a total revenue of $3,110,855 and maintains a high commitment to its mission with a program expense ratio of 96.1% [2].
